settings.title: How to Keep Your Color From Fading So Fast
settings.excerpt: Hot water and the wrong shampoo are quietly stripping the color you paid for.
settings.body:
Clients who color often ask why it dulls within weeks. A few easy changes keep it vivid far longer, and none of them cost much.

Color does not really wash out so much as it gets rinsed and bleached away by everyday habits. Fix the habits and the same color holds far better.

The three changes that matter

Turn the water down. Hot water opens the hair and lets color rinse away. Cooler water at the end seals it in, and it is kinder to your scalp besides.

Wait before the first wash. Give fresh color a couple of days to set before shampooing. Those first forty-eight hours are when most avoidable fading happens.

Skip the harsh shampoo. Anything with sulphates strips pigment. A gentle, color-safe formula makes a real difference, and we are happy to point you to one.

A few more small things

If you want to squeeze out every extra week:

  • Wash less often. Two or three times a week is plenty for most.
  • Rinse off chlorine and salt water promptly, as both are hard on color.
  • Use a heat protectant before straighteners or tongs.

Do most of these and you will stretch the time between visits noticeably. Ask at your next appointment and we will tailor the advice to your exact shade and hair type.
